services.name: Chatbox Frontend
services.technology: Chatbox fork (Electron + React + Vite + TypeScript), Mantine v7, TanStack Router/Query, Jotai
services.description: Student chat UI, Mission Control dashboard, admin portal. Electron for desktop, web build (CHATBOX_BUILD_PLATFORM=web) for Railway deployment. Renders app cards as sandboxed iframes inline in chat thread. Grade-band adaptive UI via CSS variables.
services.scaling: Static web build served by Fastify or nginx on Railway

services.name: Fastify API Server
services.description: REST API + WebSocket endpoints. Routes: auth, chat, apps, cbp, collab, classrooms, districts, analytics, consent, admin, lti, health. Middleware: RBAC, RLS tenant context, rate limiting, audit logging. Auto-OpenAPI via @fastify/swagger.
services.scaling: Node.js cluster mode (4 workers), auto-scale on Railway. WebSocket connections balanced across workers via Redis Pub/Sub.

services.name: Content Safety Pipeline
services.description: 4-stage sequential pipeline: PII detection (regex, <50ms) → injection detection (regex, <20ms) → LLM classification (Claude Sonnet, <500ms) → crisis detection (keyword, <10ms). Total budget <600ms. Runs on every message crossing trust boundaries.
services.scaling: Co-located; stateless invocation. LLM classification is the bottleneck (API call).

services.name: AI Service
services.description: LLM orchestration: streaming chat responses, tool use (app invocation), state analysis (chess position), suggestion generation. Dynamic tool schema injection from enabled classroom apps. Context window management. inference_geo='us' for COPPA.
services.scaling: Co-located with API server. Async I/O handles concurrent LLM calls.

services.name: CBP Bridge Handler
services.description: ChatBridge Bridge Protocol: JSON-RPC 2.0 over postMessage. Origin validation, schema validation (Zod, 64KB limit), PII stripping on outbound payloads. Dispatch via Redis Pub/Sub to WebSocket relay.

services.name: Collaboration Engine
services.description: Server-authoritative event sourcing for collaborative sessions. Turn enforcement (chess). State sync via Redis Pub/Sub. Session codes for joining. Teacher observation and close.
services.scaling: Co-located; Redis handles cross-worker state relay.

services.name: PostgreSQL 16
services.description: Primary data store. RLS for multi-tenant isolation (SET LOCAL per transaction). Tables: users, districts, schools, classrooms, conversations, messages, apps, district_app_catalog, app_instances, collaborative_sessions, session_participants, consent_records, audit_events, analytics_events.
services.scaling: Railway managed. Connection pool via Prisma (pool_size=10, max_overflow=5).

services.name: Redis 7
services.description: Pub/Sub for WebSocket fan-out (per-classroom channels, CBP command channels, Mission Control channels). Session cache. Rate limiting (sliding window). Collaborative session state relay.
services.scaling: Railway managed. Single instance sufficient for V1 scale (3K concurrent connections).

services.name: Langfuse (self-hosted)
services.technology: Langfuse Docker image, Railway container, dedicated PostgreSQL
services.description: LLM observability. Traces every AI call with: prompt, response, latency, tokens, cost, model, tools, guardrails. Pseudonymous user context (no PII). US data residency via self-hosted on Railway US region.
services.scaling: Separate Railway service with dedicated Postgres.

trust_boundaries.name: Authenticated Platform
trust_boundaries.description: JWT-authenticated requests. RLS tenant context set. Role validated.

trust_boundaries.name: Iframe Sandbox
trust_boundaries.description: Third-party app code. Sandboxed iframe. Communication only via CBP postMessage with origin validation.

trust_boundaries.name: Data Layer
trust_boundaries.description: PostgreSQL (RLS enforced) + Redis. Internal network only.

trust_boundaries.name: External APIs
trust_boundaries.description: Anthropic, Spotify, OpenWeatherMap, LTI platforms. Outbound only.

adrs.title: Monoglot TypeScript with Fastify backend
adrs.context: Chatbox frontend is TypeScript/React. Backend could be Python/FastAPI (reference repo) or TypeScript/Fastify.
adrs.decision: TypeScript/Fastify for backend. Monoglot stack with shared Zod schemas.

positive: Same AI SDK frontend and backend
negative: Reference Python backend must be rewritten
Less mature ML ecosystem than Python
Prisma RLS requires raw SQL workaround

adrs.title: PostgreSQL RLS for multi-tenant isolation
adrs.context: FERPA requires zero cross-district data leakage. Options: RLS, database-per-tenant, app-layer filtering.
adrs.decision: PostgreSQL RLS with SET LOCAL per-transaction via Prisma middleware.

negative: Prisma requires raw SQL for SET LOCAL

adrs.title: CBP: JSON-RPC 2.0 over postMessage extending MCP
adrs.context: Third-party apps need to communicate with the platform from sandboxed iframes. Options: custom REST, postMessage, WebSocket per-app.
adrs.decision: JSON-RPC 2.0 over postMessage with origin validation, schema enforcement, PII stripping. Extends MCP with K-12 safety primitives.

adrs.title: Redis Pub/Sub for WebSocket fan-out
adrs.context: Multi-worker Node.js needs cross-worker message broadcast. Options: Redis Pub/Sub, RabbitMQ, native cluster messaging.
adrs.decision: Redis Pub/Sub with per-classroom channels. Already in stack for caching/rate limiting.

adrs.title: Single Claude Sonnet model for all tasks
adrs.context: Could use model routing (Haiku for classification, Sonnet for chat, Opus for reasoning) or single model.
adrs.decision: Sonnet for everything. Customer chose simplicity.

negative: Higher cost than Haiku for classification
May be slower than Opus for complex reasoning

adrs.title: Server-authoritative event sourcing for collaboration (no CRDTs)
adrs.context: Collaborative sessions (chess) need state sync. Options: CRDTs, server-authoritative, optimistic with rollback.
adrs.decision: Server-authoritative via Redis Pub/Sub. Turn-based enforcement.

negative: Higher latency than CRDTs for concurrent edits (not needed for chess)

adrs.title: Langfuse self-hosted for COPPA-compliant observability
adrs.context: Need LLM observability but student data cannot leave US infrastructure. Options: Langfuse self-hosted, LangSmith, Helicone.
adrs.decision: Langfuse self-hosted on Railway. Pseudonymous IDs. US data residency.

adrs.title: LTI 1.3 Advantage via ltijs
adrs.context: Need LMS integration. Canvas and Schoology support LTI 1.3. Google Classroom does not.
adrs.decision: LTI 1.3 Advantage (SSO + AGS + NRPS) via ltijs library. Google Classroom deferred to V2.

negative: Google Classroom (24% market) not covered in V1

monorepo_structure.description: pnpm monorepo with shared packages between Chatbox frontend and Fastify backend

packages.name: @chatbridge/shared
packages.purpose: Shared Zod schemas, types, CBP protocol definitions, constants

packages.name: @chatbridge/backend
packages.purpose: Fastify API server, safety pipeline, CBP handler, collaboration engine

packages.name: @chatbridge/sdk
packages.purpose: Developer SDK for building ChatBridge apps (CBP client library)

data_flow.student_message: Browser → HTTPS → Fastify → Auth middleware (JWT) → RLS middleware (SET LOCAL) → Safety pipeline (4 stages) → AI Service (Sonnet streaming + tool use) → [if tool call] → CBP Dispatcher → Redis Pub/Sub → WebSocket → iframe app
data_flow.app_state_update: iframe app → postMessage → Frontend CBP handler → Origin validation + schema validation → WebSocket → Fastify → Safety check → Persist state → Update AI context → Broadcast to Mission Control via Redis
data_flow.teacher_whisper: Mission Control → WebSocket → Fastify → Store whisper → On next student message, inject into AI system prompt → AI response reflects whisper
data_flow.collaborative_move: Player A browser → WebSocket → Fastify → Validate turn order → Update game state → Redis Pub/Sub → All participants' WebSockets → Board updates
